name: Hozi — WhatsApp Officer Bot (district name → live brief)

meta.description: Inbound WhatsApp bot for Hozi. An officer texts a district name (or DEADLINE / DATA / COST); n8n fetches the LIVE Hozi engine data from the public app and replies with the real brief, risk, decision deadline and data verification. No secrets stored in this file — set credentials at import.

nodes.name: WhatsApp Trigger
nodes.notes: Fires when the officer sends a WhatsApp message. Uses Meta's WhatsApp Cloud API (free tier). Set the 'Hozi WhatsApp Trigger' credential and register this node's webhook URL in the Meta app dashboard. No secrets are stored in this JSON.

nodes.name: Parse Message
nodes.notes: Reads the incoming message. Detects the intent: a district name (default → brief), or the keywords COST / DEADLINE / DATA / HELP.

nodes.name: Has Message?
nodes.notes: WhatsApp also sends delivery/read receipts with no text. This gate stops those so the bot only replies to real questions.

nodes.name: Fetch Projections
nodes.notes: Fetches the LIVE engine output (risk scores, forecasts, drivers) from the public Hozi app — the exact same file the map reads. This is the linkage: the bot and the app read one source.

nodes.name: Fetch Briefs
nodes.notes: Fetches the LIVE pre-generated district briefs (en/sn/nd) from the public Hozi app — the same text the drill-down panel shows.

nodes.name: Build Reply
nodes.notes: The brain. Parses the two live JS files, matches the district the officer named, and composes the WhatsApp reply for the detected intent (brief / deadline / data / cost / help). Every number comes from the live files — nothing is hard-coded.

nodes.name: Send WhatsApp Reply
nodes.notes: Sends the reply back to the officer. Replace WHATSAPP_PHONE_NUMBER_ID_PLACEHOLDER with your Meta test number's Phone Number ID, and set the 'Hozi WhatsApp Send' credential (same Meta app). No token is stored in this JSON.
